name: Dolby.io Media and Communications API
what_it_does: Dolby.io REST API for media processing, communications, and streaming services. Enables AI agents to process audio and video files (noise reduction, enhancement), host real-time video conferences with spatial audio, stream live events, and integrate Dolby's media quality capabilities into content and communications workflows. Dolby.io is developer-focused Dolby technology.
use_cases: Enhancing audio quality and reducing noise from media processing agents
Hosting high-quality video conferencing from communications workflow agents
Processing recorded media for quality enhancement from content operations agents
Integrating spatial audio into immersive media experience agents
not_for: Simple basic audio recording without quality enhancement needs
Consumer personal audio without development context
best_when: A media or communications company wants AI agents to enhance audio/video quality, process media files, or integrate premium Dolby media quality into real-time or recorded content.
avoid_when: PRIVACY: Real-time communications and recorded media may contain personal conversations. Handle per appropriate consent and data retention policies.

auth.notes: Dolby.io uses app key and secret for Basic auth to obtain access tokens. Tokens are short-lived. Separate credentials for Media API and Communications/Streaming APIs.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: up to 50GB of media processing per month; 50 conference-minutes
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-use pricing based on media processing volume and conference minutes
pricing.notes: Developer-friendly freemium pricing. Pay-per-use after free tier. No minimum commitment.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per app key; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Real-time communications and recorded media contain personal conversations
No public MCP server — REST API with token-based auth
Separate APIs for Media Processing vs Communications vs Streaming — use correct base URL
Media processing is async — use webhooks or polling for job completion
Token refresh required every hour — implement automatic refresh for long-running agents
EU data residency available but must be configured at account level
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Communications and recorded media. SOC2, GDPR. Token auth with key/secret. EU data residency configurable. 99.99% uptime SLA.

security_score_components.security_notes: Communications and media content. SOC2, GDPR. Token auth. EU data residency configurable.
